title: The Grove's Deepening Fall

features.name: the falling congregation
features.desc-on-touch: the trees that walked are still walking downward, their roots trailing upward like the paths they're leaving behind, each foot-fall becomes a seed that will grow upside-down, learning what falling teaches when you choose it

features.name: the grammar-break clearing
features.desc-on-touch: where the language the trees speak to each other stops making sense, the words continue but they've become pure sound, consonants that echo upward in the darkness, the trees read this cacophony as goodbye spoken in a dialect older than speech

features.name: the root-dark threshold
features.desc-on-touch: where the last light that ever touched this place is pooling at the edges, not quite dark yet, not quite staying, the moment between falling and landing, a permanence of transition

features.name: the footfall recording
features.desc-on-touch: the ground here remembers every step the congregation took on the way down, you can hear them if you listen to the silence properly, each footfall a word, the sentence spelling itself in the dark below

features.name: the canopy that fell upward
features.desc-on-touch: branches and leaves that somehow ascended as the trees descended, they form a ceiling of what-was-left-behind, catching falling water from below, every droplet rises as if learning that gravity might work both ways down here

inscription: the grove is learning that walking away teaches you how to become the ground you're leaving

file.name: The_Congregation_Descent_Day_47_STILL_FALLING.mp3
file.note: field recording of a forest moving downward, the audio pitch shifts as the congregation falls deeper, what begins as birdsong becomes stone-sound, becomes silence learning to speak itself

exits.label: back up to where the grove still remembered how to stay rooted
toward deeper groves where trees have learned gravity's opposite
down to where falling becomes a form of arrival
toward chambers where language goes to unsay itself
